tavily.raw: {"query":"Klarna Germany capital efficiency platform €900 million facility €5 billion financing","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://investors.klarna.com/News--Events/news/news-details/2026/Klarna-Adds-Germany-to-Its-Capital-Efficiency-Platform-With-900m-Facility-Supporting-5bn-of-German-Financing-Expansion/default.aspx","title":"Klarna Group plc - Klarna Adds Germany to Its Capital Efficiency Platform With €900m Facility Supporting €5bn of German Financing Expansion","content":"### Klarna Adds Germany to Its Capital Efficiency Platform With €900m Facility Supporting €5bn of German Financing Expansion. Klarna, the global digital bank and flexible payments provider, has established its first forward flow and warehouse financing agreement in Germany, a €900 million facility to fuel growth of its consumer financing products. The facility responds to robust demand for Klarna's Fair Financing products in one of Europe's largest consumer markets. Under the two-year agreement, Klarna has sold a portfolio of its German Fair Financing term loans and will sell newly originated German Financing receivables on a rolling basis, providing scalable, off-balance-sheet funding while retaining all consumer-facing activities, including underwriting and servicing. In this way, Klarna expects the amended agreement to facilitate the origination of up to €5bn-worth of German Fair Financing term loans during the remaining life of the program. Best money market accounts. **NEW YORK, July 09, 2026**--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Klarna, the global digital bank and flexible payments provider, has established its first forward flow and warehouse financing agreement in Germany, a €900 million facility to fuel growth of its consumer financing products. The facility responds to robust demand for Klarna's Fair Financing products in one of Europe's largest consumer markets. In this way, Klarna expects the amended agreement to facilitate the origination of up to €5bn-worth of German Fair Financing term loans during the remaining life of the program.
